---
title: "Editable + Runnable Slides"
subtitle: "Proof of concept — two meanings of \"run\""
author: "Linguistic Data Analysis II"
format:
  live-revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../../assets/css/slides.scss]
    slide-number: true
    incremental: false
engine: jupyter
jupyter: lda2-2026
# quarto-live in-browser runtime (Pyodide). Preload keeps the packages the
# live cells need ready before the first Run click.
pyodide:
  packages:
    - pandas
    - scikit-learn
---

## Two meanings of "run"

A Quarto slide deck can execute code in **two different ways**:

1. **Render-time execution** — code runs once, when *you* build the deck.
   The audience sees baked-in output. (Standard `{python}` cell, jupyter engine.)
2. **In-browser, editable, live** — the *viewer* edits the code in the slide
   and runs it in their browser. No server. (`{pyodide}` cell, `quarto-live`.)

This deck shows one of each.

## 1 · Render-time cell {.smaller}

Computed **once at render time** with the jupyter + `uv` engine. The audience
cannot change it — the output below is frozen into the HTML.

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: fragment
from sklearn.metrics import precision_score, recall_score, f1_score

# Tiny hand-labelled example: gold vs. model predictions (1 = "error present")
gold = [1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0]
pred = [1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0]

print(f"Precision: {precision_score(gold, pred):.2f}")
print(f"Recall:    {recall_score(gold, pred):.2f}")
print(f"F1:        {f1_score(gold, pred):.2f}")
```

## 2 · Live, editable cell {.smaller}

This one runs **in your browser** via Pyodide. Edit a value, click **Run**,
and watch the output change. First run downloads the runtime (a few seconds).

::: {.callout-tip}
Try changing a prediction below from `0` to `1` and re-run.
:::

```{pyodide}
#| autorun: false
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.metrics import precision_score, recall_score, f1_score

gold = [1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0]
pred = [1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0]   # <- edit me, then click Run

df = pd.DataFrame({"gold": gold, "pred": pred})
df["correct"] = df["gold"] == df["pred"]

print(df.to_string(index=False))
print()
print(f"Accuracy:  {df['correct'].mean():.2f}")
print(f"Precision: {precision_score(gold, pred):.2f}")
print(f"Recall:    {recall_score(gold, pred):.2f}")
print(f"F1:        {f1_score(gold, pred):.2f}")
```

## When to use which {.smaller}

| | Render-time `{python}` | Live `{pyodide}` |
|---|---|---|
| Runs where | Your machine, at build | Viewer's browser |
| Editable by audience | No | **Yes** |
| Needs a server | No | No |
| Heavy libs (`spacy`) | ✅ | ⚠️ limited |
| LLM API calls | ✅ (with keys) | ❌ (no keys / CORS) |

**Takeaway:** use live cells for lightweight, hands-on demos
(pandas, metrics, small transforms). Keep `spacy` and LLM-API work in the
Colab notebooks.
